entry.text: A flat-bottomed barge lists hard to port in a reed-choked channel, its hull stove in below the waterline on the starboard side. A cargo crane arm hangs over the deck like a broken limb, and beneath it, a sealed container is still bolted to the deck — above the waterline, barely. The gangplank is gone but a rope ladder trails from the gunwale.

choices.label: Climb the rope ladder to the deck
Wade around to the lower stern hatch

deck.text: The deck is slick with algae and tilted enough that you walk with one foot on the railing. The container is a shipping box, its lock corroded to uselessness — one strike with your heel pops it. Inside is a mix of pre-war industrial supplies: lubricant cans, cable spools, and one undamaged fuel canister.

choices.label: Force the wheelhouse door for the captain's locker
Pry open the forward cargo hatch

wheelhouse.text: The wheelhouse is humid and close, every surface furred with black mould. The captain's locker holds a first-aid kit sealed in a waterproof case, a paper chart of pre-war waterways, and a half-eaten brick of compressed rations that's still sealed. You work fast — the barge groans and settles lower as you strip the last useful item.

ladder_snap.text: Two rungs from the top the ladder's top fitting tears free of the gunwale. You drop into the shallows and one leg hits the sunken hull hard.

hull_slice.text: A torn hull plate hidden beneath the waterline opens a deep gash across your calf. The sting is immediate and fierce.

list_shift.text: The barge settles another foot to port with a grinding shudder. You're thrown into the bulkhead and slide toward the open railing. You catch the crane arm and hang there until the movement stops.

gas_pocket.text: The hatch releases a pocket of trapped methane. Your eyes water and your lungs seize for a terrifying few seconds before you stagger back into open air.
